dairy: Tip: Store milk on the back shelf where temperatures stay coldest.
Tip: Wrap cheese in wax paper, then a loose bag so it can breathe.
Tip: Freeze yogurt portions for quick smoothie cubes.
Tip: Keep eggs in their carton to reduce odor transfer.
Tip: Use older dairy first by keeping newest items at the back.
produce: Tip: Keep berries dry and wash only right before eating.
Tip: Store leafy greens with a paper towel to absorb moisture.
Tip: Keep bananas away from other fruit to slow ripening.
Tip: Tomatoes keep best at room temperature until ripe.
Tip: Trim and refrigerate herbs in a glass with a little water.
meat: Tip: Freeze meat within two days if you will not cook it soon.
Tip: Defrost meat in the fridge, never on the counter.
Tip: Store raw meat on the lowest shelf to prevent drips.
Tip: Portion meat before freezing so you thaw only what you need.
Tip: Label frozen meat with date and cut for easy rotation.
bread: Tip: Slice and freeze bread so you can toast portions as needed.
Tip: Keep bread in a cool, dry cupboard away from heat.
Tip: Refresh stale bread in a warm oven for a few minutes.
Tip: Avoid refrigerating most bread; it can stale faster.
Tip: Freeze extra buns and thaw overnight to reduce waste.
leftovers: Tip: Cool leftovers quickly and refrigerate within two hours.
Tip: Store leftovers in shallow containers for faster cooling.
Tip: Add a date label so you can eat older leftovers first.
Tip: Freeze extra portions if you will not eat them in 3 days.
Tip: Reheat leftovers to steaming hot all the way through.
condiments: Tip: Refrigerate opened sauces unless label says shelf-stable.
Tip: Use clean utensils in jars to avoid contamination.
Tip: Wipe bottle rims to keep caps from getting sticky and moldy.
Tip: Date homemade dressings so they are used in time.
Tip: Keep condiments grouped by use-by date for easy rotation.
general: Tip: Plan one leftovers meal each week to use what you already have.
Tip: Put soon-to-expire items at eye level in your fridge.
Tip: Date labels help you use food in the right order.
Tip: Freeze single portions to save food and prep time.
Tip: Store similar items together so nothing gets forgotten.
